title: Credit Crunch: Xponance’s McElreath on Credit Inefficiencies
summary: “The portfolio manager’s dilemma is, ‘Do I take risk, or do I not take risk?’” Noel McElreath, senior portfolio manager for US fixed income at Xponance, tells Bloomberg Intelligence’s Noel Hebert. “And then, ‘What is excess return telling me?’” On this episode of Credit Crunch, the pair discuss the importance of disciplined credit selection, exploiting structural inefficiencies and maintaining a long-term perspective. summary: HDFC Bank Ltd.’s workforce declined by 3,343 employees in the last financial year as India’s largest private lender accelerated efforts to automate operations and redeploy staff to customer-facing roles.

title: Nippon Paint Said to Offer $8.6 Billion for Akzo Nobel Paint Arm
summary: Nippon Paint Holdings Co. has made multiple offers for Akzo Nobel NV’s decorative paints business in the past month, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: China is Supercharging a Rooftop Solar Boom in the Philippines
summary: Soaring electricity bills are pushing Filipino households and businesses to embrace solar energy, and China is cashing in.

title: Chip Selloff Hits Stocks as Oil Gains Lift Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in South Korean chipmakers drove global technology stocks lower, while oil and bond yields climbed after a fresh round of strikes in the Middle East over the weekend.

title: Investors Expect ‘Sunshine and Rainbows’ This Earnings Season
summary: Estimates are running up against sticky inflation and rising odds of interest rate hikes. With stocks near records, there’s little room for error.

title: Fed’s Bowman Urges Global Financial Watchdog to be Flexible
summary: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop warned the Financial Stability Board against enforcing “strict rules” that are not suitable for a diverse group of countries, saying those efforts can erode the international watchdog’s effectiveness.

title: Germany’s Smag Sinks in Debut as Defense Stock Appetite Ebbs
summary: Smag Mobile Antenna Masts AG shares tumbled as much as 33% below the initial public offering price in their German trading debut, marking the latest sign of waning demand for defense stocks in Europe.

title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
summary: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.

title: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Win for Prabowo
summary: Indonesia retained its investment-grade rating and stable outlook at S&P Global Ratings, a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility after a bond market selloff in June.

title: UAE Steps Up Moves to Help Term Buyers Avoid Hormuz Transits
summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is broadening the options for long-term customers to take offshore oil from outside the Strait of Hormuz, stepping up efforts that enable buyers to circumvent the chokepoint.

title: Apple’s $600 Billion Rally Fueled by Traders Fleeing AI Selloff
summary: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.

title: Maker of Soya Supreme Cooking Oil Plans Pakistan IPO This Month
summary: Cooking oil manufacturer Agro Processors & Atmospheric Gases Ltd. is planning an initial public offering in Pakistan this month to finance a plant expansion, adding to a record year for listings in the country.

title: CLO Fund’s 50% Tumble Fuels Power Struggle That Spills Into Open
summary: A fight over who gets to run a $580 million credit fund has erupted into an unusually public Wall Street brawl, pitting claims of poor performance against accusations of a cash grab ahead of a proxy vote later this month.

title: Wall Street to Warsh: Skip the Guidance, But Tell Us What You Think About the Economy
summary: In his zeal to avoid signaling where interest rates are headed, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has obscured something else that’s crucial to investors, analysts and other policymakers: How he would react when challenged by the economy.

title: Treasury Two-Year Yields Rise to Highest Since 2025 as Oil Jumps
summary: Treasury two-year yields climbed to their highest level since early 2025 as renewed tensions in Iran pushed up oil prices, fanning speculation that the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates to combat inflation.

title: Deutsche Bank Boosts Gulf Team With Standard Chartered Hires
summary: Deutsche Bank AG is expanding its private banking business in Dubai with a series of hires from Standard Chartered Plc, further deepening its presence in the Middle East despite the regional conflict.

title: European Stocks Steady as Energy Gains Offset Tech Weakness
summary: European stocks were muted as declines in technology shares were offset by gains in the energy sector, which was lifted by higher oil prices.

title: Gold and Silver Drop as Mideast Strikes Heighten Rate-Hike Risks
summary: Gold and silver tumbled after the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes over the weekend, sending energy prices higher and once more raising the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

title: Saudi Tech Firm NourNet Is Said to Pick Goldman, HSBC for IPO
summary: Saudi Arabia’s NourNet is working with Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and HSBC Holdings Plc on a potential initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a pipeline of Gulf listings despite the regional conflict.

title: India Trade Deficit Widens as Hormuz Uncertainty Clouds Outlook
summary: India’s trade deficit widened in June, with uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continuing to cloud the outlook for global supply chains.

title: Oil Rises as Conflict Over Hormuz Escalates With Fresh Strikes
summary: Oil gained after the US carried out another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open.

title: China’s Oil Imports Poised to Recover as Stockpiling Returns
summary: China’s crude imports look poised to recover from a months-long slump as the country relaxes fuel export curbs, raises run rates and snaps up prompt Middle East supplies, with analysts and traders forecasting a return to strategic stockpiling later this year.

title: James Bond Studio Pinewood in Talks on Sterling Debt Sale
summary: The UK film and TV studio behind the James Bond movies is meeting with investors ahead of a possible sale of sterling-denominated notes.

title: De Beers to Shutter South African Diamond Mine to Save Cash
summary: Diamond giant De Beers plans to halt production at its South African mine for two years, as the one-time monopoly continues to wrestle with one of the deepest crises to ever hit the industry.

title: As Milei’s Aura Fades, Argentina Starts to Look for a Third Way
summary: Corruption was once Javier Milei’s strongest card against the Peronist movement that dominated Argentine politics for decades. Now it’s just one of several vulnerabilities that are damaging the libertarian president’s brand and giving wings to the opposition’s comeback plans.

title: German Tax Trades Fallout May Cost Firms €7 Billion, BaFin Says
summary: German financial watchdog BaFin said financial companies that engaged in two controversial strategies to game dividend taxes are facing related legal and fiscal liabilities of as much €7.01 billion ($7.99 billion).

title: The Three Pillars of Graham's Legacy
summary: From Donald Trump skeptic to one of the president's closest allies, Lindsey Graham spent decades shaping US foreign policy and the Republican Party. Here's a look at the South Carolina senator's political legacy following his death at 71. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Jumps as US Conducts Wave of Strikes Against Iran
summary: Global benchmark Brent rose above $79 a barrel while West Texas Intermediate edged toward $75 after US forces completed another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TSMC’s Sales Soar 36% in Latest Sign of AI Spending Momentum
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales, meeting elevated expectations while signaling global demand for AI computing remains intact.

title: European Stocks 'Vulnerable' Without Solid Iran Deal: Schroders
summary: Remi Olu-Pitan, head of multi-asset growth and income at Schroders, discusses the outlook for US earnings and European stocks. "Europe in general tends to be the peace trade, and we're not getting a clear peace deal," Olu-Pitan tells Bloomberg Television. "It leaves Europe vulnerable." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix Shares Plunge Most on Record in Deepening Korea Selloff
summary: South Korea’s AI-fueled stock rally came under renewed pressure Monday as SK Hynix Inc. tumbled by a record 15%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom has become overstretched.

title: Akzo Nobel Rejects $8.6 Billion Nippon Offer for Paint Unit
summary: Akzo Nobel NV’s board rejected an offer from Nippon Paint Holdings Co. for its decorative paints business, and continued to recommend an agreed upon merger with Axalta Coating Systems Ltd.

title: Yen Falls on Report Japan Has No Plans to Change GPIF Allocation
summary: The yen weakened and Japanese government bonds extended losses after Reuters reported that Japan has no plans to overhaul the Government Pension Investment Fund’s asset allocation.
